figures.caption: Table 3: Real-time efficiency comparison between original model and DIET-pruned model on Gemma-2 2B. Latency is measured in milliseconds (ms), memory denotes peak GPU memory usage in megabytes (MB), and FLOPs are computed per forward pass (1012).

Table 2: Zero-shot accuracy comparisons of DIET against baseline pruning algorithms on Gemma-2 9B on seven NLP benchmarks. The best performances are marked in bold, and the runner-up is marked with underline. Tasks with * are presented with acc_norm and remaining tasks with acc.
Table 17: Basic prompts used for our evaluation (lm-eval-harness defaults).

Figure 1: Step 1. Task-wise activation profiling is performed on a dense LLM with hidden width d. Step 2. A global pruning mask is constructed via task-aware aggregation: for each task t, a binary selector selects low-importance dimensions based on activation scores. These task-wise votes are aggregated through majority voting to produce the global mask m ∈ {0, 1}d. Step 3. The mask is applied across all layers, reducing the residual width from d to d′.
Figure 2: Consensus-vote histogram by dimension across 7 tasks on Gemma-2 2B. Numbers show total counts (and share of d=2304).
Figure 3: Zero-shot accuracy by activation-profiling sample size on Gemma-2 2B across seven benchmarks. Benchmarks with * use acc_norm.
Table 23: Zero-shot accuracy comparisons on IFEval for Gemma-2 2B and 9B. All values are reported as accuracy scores.

Table 1: Zero-shot accuracy comparisons of DIET against baseline pruning algorithms on Gemma-2 2B on seven NLP benchmarks. The best performances are marked in bold, and the runner-up is marked with underline. Tasks with * are presented with acc_norm and remaining tasks with acc.
Table 18: Zero-shot accuracy of SliceGPT on Gemma-2 2B on seven NLP benchmarks, using WikiText2 and seven benchmarks. Tasks with * are presented with acc_norm and remaining tasks with acc.

Table 19: Zero-shot accuracy comparisons of DIET against baseline pruning algorithms on Gemma-2 2B on seven NLP benchmarks. The best performances are marked in bold, and the runner-up is marked with underline. Tasks with * are presented with acc_norm and remaining tasks with acc.
